DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS THOMAS H. ROBERTS, LON MURPHY, MASON-MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. fka PRUDENTIAL CALIFORNIA REALTY'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF DEAN BALLBACK, JAMES GREENHAW, 737 CASTRO STREET, PROPERTIES LLC.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Oral Breach of K) is SUSTAINED With leave to amend for failure to state facts showing breach. The Court previously granted P Leave to amend to plead the terms of the K and breach more clearly. P has not done so. FAC 24 states that Roberts breached the oral K by failing to properly manage the affairs of the LLC and the construction of the property by misappropriating and/or squandering the LLC's funds. P never alleged that such management was actually a covenant of the alleged oral K. The demurrer to the 1st cause of action on the grounds that the oral K is superseded by the Listing Agreement or Operating Agreement is OVERRULED. The Court DENIES D's RJN of the Listing Agreement. Furthermore, the Court is bound to accept Ps' interpretation of the Listing Agreement and Operating Agreement as true for matters of a demurrer. See Aragon Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Negligent Misrep) is OVERRULED. P has stated a cause of action with the requisite particularity. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Breach of Fiduciary Duty) is OVERRULED. Ps have stated that Roberts was acting in his capacity as a real estate broker, which gives rise to a fiduciary relationship. Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is SUSTAINED with Leave to amend for the same reasons as the 1st cause of action. Demurrer to the 5th cause of action is OVERRULED. An accounting is necessary to determine the amount of funds allegedly misappropriated that are not accounted for on the financial records provided to Ps in 3/08. Demurrer on SOL grounds is SUSTAINED With Leave to amend. P must specifically plead facts showing: (1) the time and manner of discovery; and (2) facts that show P's inability to have earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808. Demurrer on standing grounds is OVERRULED. (Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS THOMAS H. ROBERTS, LON MURPHY, MASON-MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. fka PRUDENTIAL CALIFORNIA REALTY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF DEAN BALLBACK, JAMES GREENHAW, 737 CASTRO STREET, PROPERTIES LLC.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE COURT DECLINES TO DETERMINE THE TRUTH OR FALSITY OF PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS ON A MOTION TO STRIKE. PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS THOMAS H. ROBERTS, LON MURPHY AND MASON-MCDUFFIE REAL ESTATE, INC. FKA PRUDENTIAL CALIFORNIA REALTY'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT OF DEAN BALLBACK, JAMES GREENHAW, 737 CASTRO STREET, PROPERTIES LLC.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD TERMS OF CONTRACT AND BREACH MORE CLEARLY.
DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD WITH MORE PARTICULARITY.
DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD THAT BREACHES AROSE FROM BROKER RELATIONSHIP OR OTHER FACTS INDICATING THE EXISTENCE OF A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP.
DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION.
DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND CONSPIRACY AS THEY ARE NOT CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD HIS CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS AS A GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATION. SEE JOHNSON V. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL (2009) 179 CAL.APP.4TH 549, 555-556; OKUN V. SUPERIOR COURT (MAPLE PROPERTIES) (1981) 29 CAL.3RD 442, 454. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
